expectations.text: Contains no technical jargon (query, B-tree, schema, SQL, optimize, table, column, row in technical sense)
expectations.evidence: No technical database terms used. Uses only everyday words like 'book', 'pages', 'list', 'puppies', 'toy', 'room'.

expectations.text: Uses at least one concrete, child-friendly analogy
expectations.evidence: Uses two strong analogies: (1) a big book with a table of contents for finding puppies, (2) finding a toy in an organized vs messy room.

expectations.text: Sentences are short and simple, average under 15 words per sentence
expectations.evidence: Sentences are consistently short: 'That would take forever, right?', 'Your arms would get so tired!', 'So much faster!'

expectations.text: Tone is warm, playful, and appropriate for a 5-year-old (not condescending)
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'Oh, this is a cool one!' Uses 'huuuge', 'super duper fast', exclamation marks. Feels like a favorite teacher explaining.
